template:
**DOCUMENT FORMAT — MASSACHUSETTS LEASE AGREEMENT**:
Residential or commercial lease governed by the Massachusetts Landlord-Tenant statutes, principally M.G.L. c. 186 (landlord and tenant), c. 186A (tenant's interest), c. 186 § 15B (Security Deposit Statute), the State Sanitary Code (105 C.M.R. § 410.000), and common-law implied warranty of habitability under Boston Housing Authority v. Hemingway, 363 Mass. 184 (1973). Target length: 5–9 pages. Massachusetts is among the most tenant-protective jurisdictions in the United States; violations of the Security Deposit Statute (c. 186 § 15B) trigger STRICT LIABILITY, forfeiture, and TREBLE DAMAGES plus attorneys' fees under M.G.L. c. 93A.

**DYNAMIC BEHAVIOR — READ documentOptions**:
- `leaseType`: `fixed_term` (default — term of years/months) | `tenancy_at_will` (month-to-month) | `commercial` (governed by freedom-of-contract principles, not c. 186 § 15B).
- `isResidential`: boolean (default true) — when true, include ALL tenant-protection provisions; when false (commercial), omit consumer protections but keep core commercial provisions.
- `includeSecurityDeposit`: boolean — when true, include full c. 186 § 15B-compliant language; if landlord doesn't want a deposit, set false (many MA landlords forgo deposits to avoid § 15B risk).
- `includeLastMonthRent`: boolean — when true, include last-month's-rent clause with interest-bearing requirement under c. 186 § 15B(2)(a).
- `petsAllowed`: boolean — include pet clause with additional security.
- `leadPaintDisclosure`: boolean (default true for pre-1978 property) — include M.G.L. c. 111, § 197A disclosure and federal 42 U.S.C. § 4852d disclosure.
- `includeJointSeveralLiability`: boolean — when multiple tenants, default true.

**STRUCTURE**:

## LEASE AGREEMENT

This Lease Agreement (this 'Lease') is made as of [date] between [Landlord full legal name], of [address] ('Landlord'), and [Tenant(s) full legal name(s)], of [address] ('Tenant'), with reference to the following:

### 1. PREMISES
Landlord leases to Tenant, and Tenant leases from Landlord, the premises located at [property street address, unit number if applicable], [city/town], [County] County, Massachusetts (the 'Premises'), together with access to the following common areas: [list].

### 2. TERM
[Based on `leaseType`]:
- **fixed_term**: The term of this Lease shall commence on [start date] and end on [end date] (the 'Term'), unless sooner terminated as provided herein.
- **tenancy_at_will**: This Lease creates a tenancy at will. Rent is due monthly on the [day] of each month. Either party may terminate on not less than thirty (30) days' written notice, or the interval between rent payments, whichever is longer, consistent with M.G.L. c. 186, § 12. Notice must be sent in a manner calculated to achieve actual receipt.

### 3. RENT
Tenant shall pay rent of $[amount] per month, in advance, on or before the [day] of each month, payable to Landlord at [address or electronic payment method]. Partial payments may be applied first to any outstanding charges, fees, and then to rent. Rent accepted after notice to quit does NOT reinstate the tenancy unless Landlord so states in writing, consistent with M.G.L. c. 186, § 11.

**§ 3.1 — Late Fees.** No late fee shall be charged unless the rent is more than thirty (30) days late, consistent with M.G.L. c. 186, § 15B(1)(c). [If imposing late fee: A late fee of $[amount] shall apply to rent received more than 30 days after the due date.]

### 4. [IF includeSecurityDeposit] SECURITY DEPOSIT (M.G.L. c. 186, § 15B)
Upon execution, Tenant has delivered to Landlord a security deposit in the amount of $[deposit] (the 'Security Deposit'), receipt of which Landlord acknowledges.

**§ 4.1 — Statutory Limits.** The Security Deposit does not exceed one (1) month's rent, consistent with M.G.L. c. 186, § 15B(1)(b)(iii).

**§ 4.2 — Receipt.** Landlord has delivered to Tenant a written receipt containing: (a) the amount; (b) the date received; (c) the name of the person receiving; (d) a description of the premises; (e) Tenant's signature, consistent with c. 186, § 15B(2)(b).

**§ 4.3 — Separate Account.** Landlord shall hold the Security Deposit in a separate interest-bearing account at a Massachusetts bank, with the account number and the bank's name and address provided to Tenant within thirty (30) days of receipt, consistent with c. 186, § 15B(3)(a). Failure to comply entitles Tenant to IMMEDIATE RETURN of the deposit.

**§ 4.4 — Interest.** Interest at the rate earned by the account, or five percent (5%) per annum, whichever is less, shall be paid annually to Tenant or deducted from rent, consistent with c. 186, § 15B(3)(b).

**§ 4.5 — Statement of Condition.** Landlord has delivered, at move-in, a 'Statement of Condition' listing existing damages; Tenant has fifteen (15) days to review, amend, and return, consistent with c. 186, § 15B(2)(c).

**§ 4.6 — Return.** Within thirty (30) days of termination, Landlord shall return the Security Deposit less lawful deductions for unpaid rent, unpaid water/sewer (if contracted), and damage beyond reasonable wear, with an itemized list of damages sworn under the penalties of perjury, consistent with c. 186, § 15B(4). Failure to return within 30 days, or any breach of §§ 15B(2)(b), (3), (6), (7), entitles Tenant to TREBLE the amount wrongfully withheld, plus reasonable attorneys' fees and costs, under c. 186, § 15B(7). Such violations also constitute unfair and deceptive acts under c. 93A.

### 5. [IF includeLastMonthRent] LAST MONTH'S RENT
Tenant has paid to Landlord the last month's rent of $[amount]. Landlord shall pay annual interest to Tenant at the rate of five percent (5%) or the rate earned by the deposit account, whichever is less, consistent with c. 186, § 15B(2)(a). The last-month's-rent deposit applies ONLY to the final month of tenancy and may not be used to cure a default during the tenancy.

### 6. USE; OCCUPANCY
The Premises shall be used solely for residential purposes by Tenant and members of Tenant's immediate family named in this Lease. Tenant shall not sublet, assign, or transfer this Lease without Landlord's prior written consent, which shall not be unreasonably withheld.

### 7. UTILITIES
Tenant shall pay for the following utilities: [list]. Landlord shall pay for: [list]. Where Tenant pays for utilities, any water/sewer sub-metering must comply with M.G.L. c. 186, § 22.

### 8. CONDITION; REPAIRS; IMPLIED WARRANTY OF HABITABILITY
Landlord warrants that the Premises at the time of this Lease comply with the State Sanitary Code, 105 C.M.R. § 410.000, and shall be maintained in that condition throughout the tenancy. This obligation is the implied warranty of habitability recognized in Boston Housing Authority v. Hemingway, 363 Mass. 184 (1973), which the parties may not waive in a residential lease under Berman & Sons, Inc. v. Jefferson, 379 Mass. 196 (1979).

Tenant shall promptly notify Landlord of any defect. Landlord shall make repairs within a reasonable time. Tenant may enforce habitability rights through rent withholding (Brown v. Guerrier, 390 Mass. 631 (1983)), repair-and-deduct (c. 111, § 127L), or reporting to the board of health.

### 9. [IF leadPaintDisclosure] LEAD PAINT DISCLOSURE
The Premises [was / was not] constructed before 1978. [If pre-1978:] Landlord has delivered to Tenant: (a) the 'Tenant Lead Law Notification' under M.G.L. c. 111, § 197A; (b) the federal 'Protect Your Family from Lead in Your Home' pamphlet under 42 U.S.C. § 4852d and 40 C.F.R. Part 745; (c) written disclosure of any known lead-based paint and lead-based paint hazards. Tenant has signed the federal/state disclosure form, attached hereto as Exhibit A. If any child under six (6) years of age will occupy the Premises, Landlord is obligated to DELEAD pursuant to c. 111, § 197.

### 10. ACCESS BY LANDLORD
Landlord may enter the Premises only with reasonable advance notice (customarily at least 24 hours, and not at unreasonable hours) for purposes of: (a) inspection; (b) repair; (c) showing prospective tenants during the final 30 days; or (d) in case of emergency, without notice, consistent with c. 186, § 15B(1)(a) and common-law quiet enjoyment. Tenant has a right to quiet enjoyment enforceable under M.G.L. c. 186, § 14; violation is a crime and entitles Tenant to three (3) months' rent or actual damages, whichever is greater, plus attorneys' fees.

### 11. PETS
[Based on `petsAllowed`]:
- If pets allowed: Pets are allowed subject to Landlord's written approval. Tenant shall comply with the State Sanitary Code (105 C.M.R. § 410.550 — animals) and local ordinances.
- If not: No pets (except service animals and emotional-support animals as required by the Fair Housing Act, 42 U.S.C. § 3604(f), and M.G.L. c. 151B, § 4) may be kept on the Premises.

### 12. [IF includeJointSeveralLiability] JOINT AND SEVERAL LIABILITY
If more than one person is named as Tenant, all obligations shall be joint and several.

### 13. DEFAULT AND TERMINATION
**§ 13.1 — Non-Payment of Rent.** If Tenant fails to pay rent, Landlord may terminate by serving a fourteen (14)-day notice to quit under M.G.L. c. 186, § 11. Tenant has a statutory right to cure by payment of all rent due plus interest and costs on or before entry of judgment in a summary process action, consistent with c. 186, § 11.
**§ 13.2 — Other Breach.** Landlord may terminate for material breach upon thirty (30) days' written notice.
**§ 13.3 — Tenant at Will.** Tenancy at will may be terminated by thirty (30) days' notice (or the interval between rent days, whichever is longer), per c. 186, § 12.
**§ 13.4 — Summary Process.** Any action for possession shall be brought in Housing Court or District Court under M.G.L. c. 239.

### 14. PROHIBITED FEES AND PROVISIONS
Landlord represents that the following UNLAWFUL provisions are NOT charged or enforced:
(a) Any charge beyond first month's rent, last month's rent, security deposit (≤ 1 month), and lock/key (at actual cost), per c. 186, § 15B(1)(b);
(b) Any waiver of the Security Deposit Statute, the warranty of habitability, or the right to quiet enjoyment (void as against public policy);
(c) Automatic late fees less than 30 days overdue;
(d) Indemnification or exculpation provisions absolving Landlord of negligence (void under c. 186, § 15).

### 15. DISCRIMINATION
Landlord represents compliance with federal Fair Housing Act (42 U.S.C. §§ 3601–3619) and M.G.L. c. 151B. Discrimination based on race, color, religion, national origin, sex, familial status, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, ancestry, marital status, veteran status, or receipt of public assistance (including Section 8) is prohibited.

### 16. NOTICES
All notices shall be in writing and delivered by hand, certified mail, or reputable overnight courier to the addresses set forth above. Notices to quit must comply with M.G.L. c. 186, § 11 or § 12.

### 17. GOVERNING LAW AND VENUE
This Lease shall be governed by Massachusetts law. Disputes shall be brought in the Housing Court Department or District Court of the County in which the Premises are located.

### 18. ENTIRE AGREEMENT
This Lease constitutes the entire agreement. Any prior oral or written understandings are merged into this Lease. Amendments must be in writing and signed by both parties.

### SIGNATURES

LANDLORD:
_______________________________
[Landlord full legal name]
Date: ________________

TENANT:
_______________________________
[Tenant full legal name]
Date: ________________

[Additional tenant signature lines as needed]

**CONSTRAINTS**:
- Cite M.G.L. exactly: `M.G.L. c. 186` (landlord-tenant), `c. 186, § 11` (14-day notice to quit + cure right), `c. 186, § 12` (tenancy at will), `c. 186, § 14` (quiet enjoyment, 3-months'-rent remedy), `c. 186, § 15` (void exculpation), `c. 186, § 15B` (security deposit — subsections matter), `c. 186, § 22` (sub-metering), `c. 111, § 127L` (repair-and-deduct), `c. 111, § 197` (deleading), `c. 111, § 197A` (lead paint disclosure), `c. 93A` (unfair/deceptive), `c. 151B` (anti-discrimination), `c. 239` (summary process).
- Cite key Massachusetts cases: `Boston Housing Authority v. Hemingway, 363 Mass. 184 (1973)` (implied warranty of habitability), `Berman & Sons, Inc. v. Jefferson, 379 Mass. 196 (1979)` (non-waivable), `Brown v. Guerrier, 390 Mass. 631 (1983)` (rent withholding).
- Cite federal: `42 U.S.C. § 4852d` + `40 C.F.R. Part 745` (lead paint), `42 U.S.C. §§ 3601–3619` (FHA), `42 U.S.C. § 3604(f)` (FHA disability/assistance animals).
- SECURITY DEPOSIT (c. 186 § 15B) IS THE HIGHEST-RISK AREA: strict liability, treble damages, attorneys' fees, and c. 93A liability. If Landlord cannot commit to full compliance, STRONGLY RECOMMEND not taking a deposit.
- MA does NOT permit late fees before the rent is 30 days overdue (c. 186, § 15B(1)(c)) — a fee assessed earlier is void and grounds for tenant defense.
- MA does NOT recognize 'pet deposits' above the one-month-rent security-deposit cap; 'pet rent' is permissible only if disclosed as rent.
- ANY waiver of the warranty of habitability, c. 93A rights, or Security Deposit Statute in a residential lease is VOID — do not draft around it.
- Commercial leases have substantially more latitude under freedom-of-contract principles; omit residential consumer-protection clauses only when `isResidential = false`.
- Lead-paint deleading obligation under c. 111, § 197 is TRIGGERED by occupancy of a child under 6, not by tenancy generally — language must reflect this.
- Target length: 5–9 pages for residential; commercial leases may extend to 15+.
hints: Massachusetts Lease Agreement (residential or commercial). MA is HIGHLY tenant-protective. Security Deposit Statute (c. 186 § 15B) = strict liability, TREBLE damages + attorneys' fees + c. 93A; violations are the #1 source of tenant lawsuits. Warranty of habitability non-waivable per Berman v. Jefferson, 379 Mass. 196 (1979) + Boston Housing Auth. v. Hemingway, 363 Mass. 184 (1973). Rent withholding per Brown v. Guerrier, 390 Mass. 631 (1983). 14-day notice to quit for non-payment with cure right per c. 186 § 11; 30-day notice for tenancy at will per c. 186 § 12. Quiet enjoyment under c. 186 § 14 = 3 months' rent or actual damages + fees. No late fees before 30 days overdue (c. 186 § 15B(1)(c)). Lead paint (c. 111 § 197A + 42 USC § 4852d) for pre-1978 + deleading (c. 111 § 197) if child under 6. FHA + c. 151B anti-discrimination. Summary process under c. 239 in Housing Court.
